ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Agenda) GPO-pushed PowerShell Chrome credential harvester
ucs.description: Hunts Qilin's signature mass-credential heist tradecraft (publicly documented by Sophos X-Ops, July 2024): a PowerShell script delivered via Group Policy logon-script / SYSVOL that copies Chrome's `Login Data` SQLite database and `Local State` JSON, extracts the DPAPI-protected `encrypted_key`, and exfils browser passwords from every domain-joined endpoint before encryption. This GPO-as-credential-distribution-channel is highly distinctive and rare outside Qilin/Agenda ransomware intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name values(Processes.user) as user dc(Processes.dest) as host_count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Local State*" OR Processes.process="*Login Data*" OR (Processes.process="*encrypted_key*" AND Processes.process="*FromBase64String*") OR (Processes.process="*ProtectedData*" AND Processes.process="*DPAPI*") OR Processes.process="*AppData\\Local\\Google\\Chrome*Login Data*") AND (Processes.parent_process_name="gpscript.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="svchost.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="wmiprvse.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="taskeng.exe" OR Processes.parent_process="*\\SYSVOL\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="schtasks.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where host_count >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ChromeArtifacts = dynamic([@"\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Local State", @"\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Default\\Login Data", @"\\AppData\\Local\\Google\\Chrome", "Login Data", "Local State"]);
let DpapiTokens   = dynamic(["ProtectedData", "Unprotect", "DPAPI", "encrypted_key", "AesGcm", "System.Security.Cryptography"]);
let GpoParents    = dynamic(["gpscript.exe", "svchost.exe", "wmiprvse.exe", "taskeng.exe", "schtasks.exe", "services.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$" or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\SYSVOL\"   // keep SYSTEM context if it came from a GPO logon script
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (ChromeArtifacts)
      or (ProcessCommandLine has_any (DpapiTokens) and ProcessCommandLine has "Chrome")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (GpoParents)
      or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\SYSVOL\", @"\NETLOGON\", "gpt.ini", "GroupPolicy")
      or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\SYSVOL\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          SHA256
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            HostCount = dcount(DeviceName),
            Devices   = make_set(DeviceName, 50),
            SampleCmd = any(ChildCmd)
            by ParentImage, ChildImage
| where HostCount >= 2     // GPO-distributed payload — fans out across the estate
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ChromeArtifacts = dynamic([@"\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Local State", @"\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Default\\Login Data", "Login Data", "Local State", @"\\AppData\\Local\\Google\\Chrome"]);
let DpapiTokens     = dynamic(["ProtectedData", "Unprotect", "DPAPI", "encrypted_key", "AesGcm"]);
let GpoParents      = dynamic(["gpscript.exe", "svchost.exe", "wmiprvse.exe", "taskeng.exe", "schtasks.exe", "services.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688                              // process create — requires "Include command line" GPO
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any (ChromeArtifacts)
      or (CommandLine has_any (DpapiTokens) and CommandLine has "Chrome")
| where ParentProcessName has_any (GpoParents)
      or CommandLine has_any (@"\SYSVOL\", @"\NETLOGON\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            HostCount = dcount(Computer),
            Hosts     = make_set(Computer, 50),
            SampleCmd = any(CommandLine)
            by ParentProcessName, NewProcessName
| where HostCount >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Qilin (Agenda) GPO-Distributed Chrome Credential Harvester
id: 5b3d6e1c-7c2b-4d59-aef0-5e7d1f6a91d4
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell launched from a GPO/logon-script context that reads Chrome's Login Data or Local State, characteristic of Qilin/Agenda mass-credential staging.
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
references:
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/08/22/qilin-ransomware-caught-stealing-credentials-stored-in-google-chrome/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  selection_chrome:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\Local State'
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Login Data'
      - 'Login Data'
      - 'Local State'
      - 'encrypted_key'
  selection_parent_or_path:
    - ParentImage|endswith:
        - '\gpscript.exe'
        - '\wmiprvse.exe'
        - '\taskeng.exe'
        - '\schtasks.exe'
    - CommandLine|contains:
        - '\SYSVOL\'
        - '\NETLOGON\'
    - ParentCommandLine|contains:
        - '\SYSVOL\'
        - 'GroupPolicy'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_chrome and selection_parent_or_path
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Authorised internal credential-audit / browser-migration tooling distributed via GPO
level: high
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1037.001
ucs.rationale: Generic 'PowerShell touches Chrome' detections are noisy. What makes this Qilin-specific is the *delivery vector*: the credential-stealer is pushed estate-wide via a Group Policy logon script (parent = gpscript.exe / SYSVOL path / wmiprvse.exe under SYSTEM) and fans out to many endpoints simultaneously. This combination — DPAPI/Chrome-credential code paths in PowerShell, GPO/SYSVOL parent context,

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Agenda) Linux/ESXi Rust encryptor pre-encryption VM-kill chain
ucs.description: Hunts Qilin's ESXi/Linux variant — a Rust-compiled binary that enumerates virtual machines via `esxcli`/`vim-cmd`, force-kills running guests, then walks `/vmfs/volumes/` encrypting `.vmdk`/`.vmx`/`.vswp` files. The combination of `esxcli vm process kill` followed within minutes by mass file rename/encryption under `/vmfs/volumes` from an unsigned binary in `/tmp` is a Qilin/Agenda hallmark and not a normal ESXi admin workflow.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.os="Linux" AND ((Processes.process_name="esxcli" AND Processes.process="*vm process kill*" AND Processes.process="*--type=force*") OR (Processes.process_name="vim-cmd" AND Processes.process="*vmsvc/getallvms*") OR (Processes.process="*/tmp/*" AND (Processes.process="*-yes*" OR Processes.process="*--debug*" OR Processes.process="*--logs*" OR Processes.process="*-path*/vmfs/volumes*")) OR (Processes.process_name="chmod" AND Processes.process="*+x*" AND Processes.process="*/tmp/*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(process_name) as distinct_actions by dest | where distinct_actions >= 2 | sort 0 -firstTime | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackHours = 6h;
let KillVm   = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where DeviceName has_any ("esxi", "vmhost") or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has "/bin/" or FolderPath has "/bin/"
    | where (FileName =~ "esxcli" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("vm", "process", "kill"))
         or (FileName =~ "vim-cmd" and ProcessCommandLine has "vmsvc/getallvms");
let Encryptor = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where (FolderPath startswith "/tmp/" or FolderPath startswith "/var/tmp/" or FolderPath startswith "/vmfs/volumes/")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-yes", "--debug", "--logs", "--no-vm-kill", "-path", "--password")
         or ProcessCommandLine has "/vmfs/volumes";
KillVm
| project KillTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, KillCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner (
    Encryptor
    | project EncTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, EncImage = FolderPath, EncCmd = ProcessCommandLine, EncSHA256 = SHA256
  ) on DeviceId
| where EncTime between (KillTime .. KillTime + 30m)
| project DeviceName, KillTime, KillCmd, EncTime, EncImage, EncCmd, EncSHA256,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', EncTime, KillTime)
| order by EncTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackHours = 6h;
let KillVm = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where DvcOs has_any ("linux", "esxi") or DvcHostname has_any ("esxi", "vmhost")
    | where (TargetProcessName endswith "esxcli" and TargetProcessCommandLine has_all ("vm", "process", "kill"))
         or (TargetProcessName endswith "vim-cmd" and TargetProcessCommandLine has "vmsvc/getallvms")
    | project KillTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, KillCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername;
let Encryptor = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where TargetProcessName matches regex @"^/(tmp|var/tmp|vmfs/volumes)/.+"
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("-yes", "--debug", "--logs", "--no-vm-kill", "-path", "--password")
         or TargetProcessCommandLine has "/vmfs/volumes"
    | project EncTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, EncImage = TargetProcessName, EncCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine, EncSHA256 = SHA256;
KillVm
| join kind=inner Encryptor on DvcHostname
| where EncTime between (KillTime .. KillTime + 30m)
| project DvcHostname, ActorUsername, KillTime, KillCmd, EncTime, EncImage, EncCmd, EncSHA256,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', EncTime, KillTime)
| order by EncTime desc
ucs.rationale: Qilin (alongside affiliates of Akira and BlackBasta) is one of the few crews that ships a *Rust-compiled* ESXi encryptor with the documented `-yes / --logs / --debug / --no-vm-kill / -path` flag set and a built-in VM-killer that calls `esxcli vm process kill --type=force` before encrypting `/vmfs/volumes/`. The two-stage chain (esxcli VM kill → unsigned `/tmp` binary writing under `/vmfs/volumes`
